key_insight: W–B nanoclusters show highly composition-dependent stability and can catalyze the first step of ammonia decomposition, with several “magic” cluster sizes standing out as especially stable.
engineering_relevance: These clusters offer a tunable, Pt-like catalytic platform for ammonia decomposition, where activity can be engineered by choosing compositions and local atomic environments.
method: Evolutionary algorithm + DFT used to search WmBn ground-state nanocluster structures up to 43 atoms.
Adsorption calculations evaluated NH3 and H binding across compositions and sites.
Nudged elastic band simulations estimated the first N–H cleavage barrier.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports isolated stability maxima at WB16, W2B8, W7B24, and W11B22, NH3 adsorption energies of -0.54 to -1.78 eV, and N–H bond-cleavage barriers of 1.1–1.4 eV.
watch_item: Catalytic performance is inferred from simulations on finite clusters, so real behavior may change with support effects, temperature, and cluster reconstruction under reaction conditions.
paper_title: Insights of Ammonia Decomposition on W--B Nanoclusters by Computational Simulations
